headline: Is Verizon Communications (VZ) Offering An Opportunity After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether Verizon Communications at around US$48.62 is offering fair value or an opportunity, it helps to break the story into recent price moves, news flow, and a clear view of what the current valuation signals actually say. The stock has pulled back over the last week and month, with returns of 3.1% and 4.9% declines, while still sitting on gains of 20.0% year to date and 23.2% over the last year, as well as 48.6% over three years and 13.4% over five years. Recent...

headline: Verizon vs. AT&T: One Telecom Dividend Is on Shaky Ground
summary: Verizon (NYSE:VZ) and AT&T (NYSE:T) have both reported full-year results, and the dividend story between them is more nuanced than raw payout numbers suggest. Verizon’s check is larger, but AT&T’s balance sheet is cleaning up faster. Which dividend is actually safer requires looking past the yield. Verizon Grows Steadily. AT&T Rebuilds With Ambition. headline: Global Chaos, AI Fears Help Tech’s Dividend Stocks Beat Market
summary: (Bloomberg) -- An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks.After a dismal 2025, telecom is one of this year’s top performing sectors in the S&P 500 Index, rising more than 7% while the broad benchmark is down about 1%. headline: Verizon Cut to Hold at DBS Bank: Is the Telecom Giant Running Out of Upside?
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) received a cautious reassessment on April 8 when DBS Bank downgraded Verizon stock from “Buy” to “Hold,” setting a price target of $52. The move signals that one of Verizon’s more optimistic backers now sees the stock as fairly valued after a strong run. For income investors riding Verizon’s momentum, it’s worth ... headline: High Dividends Turn Telecom Stocks Into Market’s New Haven Play
summary: (Bloomberg) -- An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks.After a dismal 2025, telecom is one of this year’s top performing sectors in the S&P 500 Index, rising 9.6% while the broad benchmark is down 3.3%. Shares of Verizon Communication Inc. are up more than 19% in 2026, and AT&T Inc. has gained 13%. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq 100 Index fell into a correction in March, and a Bloomb

headline: Carriers swap discounts for targeted promos to boost ARPU, convergence, and retention
summary: In March, telcos, OEMs, and retailers rolled out 1,345 smartphone promos. Trade-ins, bundles, and new line offers led. The iPhone 17e launch fuelled fresh deals for switchers, new customers, and upgraders.
